---
title: "Module 03: Trees and Two Cultures"
---

## Learning objectives

- Contrast **statistical** (model-first, uncertainty central) and **algorithmic** (prediction-first, flexible learners) cultures — and why good practice blends them.
- Explain **decision trees** as recursive partitioning: splits, impurity, depth, and min-leaf-size as complexity knobs.
- Connect tree **geometry** (axis-aligned boxes in feature space) to what students see in `rpart.plot` on penguins.

## Key messages

- Trees are a **bridge**: interpretable splits today, ensembles and boosting tomorrow.
- The same **Palmer Penguins** table supports both **logistic** intuition (Monday) and **tree + tidymodels** workflows (Tuesday).

## Concept outline

### Two cultures (compressed)

- Breiman-style framing: different priorities on **assumptions** vs **error on future data**; both care about **leakage** and **evaluation**.

### Tree mechanics

- Greedy binary splits; Gini / entropy; stopping vs pruning.
- **Interactions** are automatic: sequential splits encode feature combinations without hand-written `x1:x2`.

### When trees shine or struggle

- Shine: nonlinear boundaries, mixed types, monotone-ish effects without manual basis expansion.
- Struggle: very high noise, tiny *n* without regularization via depth / `min_n` / ensembles.

## Course links

- **Slides:** [Tuesday — Trees and pipelines](../slides/day-02-tuesday.qmd)
- **Notebooks — penguins:** [Adelie vs Gentoo](../notebooks/penguins-classification.Rmd) (`rpart` + logistic boundary sketch), [three species](../notebooks/penguins-species-multiclass.Rmd), [sex](../notebooks/penguins-sex-classification.Rmd)
- **Data:** [Palmer Penguins](../dataset-penguins.qmd)
- **Next module:** [Module 04 — Tidymodels pipeline](module-04-pipeline.qmd) (full tree tuning example)
- **Module library:** [Back to all modules](index.qmd)

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
## Draft / TODO

- One **hand-drawn style** figure of 2D partition (optional asset) or link to whiteboard photo policy from instructors.
- Cross-link to **`workflowsets`** / **`stacks`** bullets on Tuesday deck when students are ready for capstone comparisons.
:::
